sector: Global Capability Centers — India GCC & Captive Shared Services
source_verification_note: All industry_sources entries have been verified against published reports as of May 2026. Only confirmed publications with real URLs or known annual series are cited.

condition_precedents: Process inventory mapped (60+ days)
RPA CoE or automation team identified
IT governance sign-off on bot deployment
industry_sources: Gartner — Market Guide for Hyperautomation Service Providers (gartner.com/en/documents/4983531)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)
Everest Group — Beyond Boundaries: GCCs Shaping Tomorrow's Enterprises (December 2024)

execution_playbook.step: Run process discovery sessions across F&A, HR ops, and IT service desk; rank automation candidates by volume × handling time × error rate
Establish CoE governance: bot ownership model, security standards, change management, and exception-handling SLA
Build and deploy Wave 1 bots (F&A reconciliation, HR onboarding, IT ticket routing); run parallel for 2 weeks before cutover
Scale to Wave 2 (invoice processing, payroll audit, report generation); decommission manual processes; track FTE release

diagnostic_signals.signal: High-volume repeatable processes (>500 transactions/day) with manual touchpoints >60% and error rate >2%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: RPA automation ROI positive within 12 months

diagnostic_signals.signal: FTE effort on rule-based tasks >30% of total FTE capacity in F&A or HR ops functions
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will release FTE capacity for higher-value work

diagnostic_signals.signal: Technology landscape includes ERP (SAP/Oracle) and ITSM (ServiceNow) with accessible APIs or UI layers for bot integration
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Technical feasibility for RPA deployment is confirmed

required_data_fields: Process inventory with transaction volumes, FTE-hours, error rates, and system touchpoints
IT application landscape with API/UI accessibility assessment
CoE governance model and bot ownership framework
Security and data-privacy requirements for automated processes
FTE cost by function and process to calculate automation ROI

condition_precedents: Attrition rate benchmarked vs sector median
Exit interview data available (6 months)
HR leadership sponsorship confirmed
industry_sources: Deloitte — 2025 Global Human Capital Trends (deloitte.com/us/en/insights/topics/talent/human-capital-trends/2025.html)
Mercer — Total Remuneration Survey, India Edition (imercer.com/products/total-remuneration-surveys)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Analyse attrition data by band, function, tenure cohort, and manager to identify root causes (compensation, career, manager quality)
Benchmark total compensation (base + variable + benefits) against NASSCOM/Aon Hewitt survey data by role and experience band
Design and implement retention interventions: compensation correction for at-risk bands, career path transparency, and manager effectiveness program
Track attrition rate by band monthly; measure regression (3-year attrition cost: recruitment + training + productivity ramp)

diagnostic_signals.signal: Attrition rate >20% for mid-level (L3–L5) employees where replacement cost is 1.5× annual CTC
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Retention improvement will yield significant avoidable recruitment and onboarding cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Exit survey data: compensation or career growth cited by >40% of voluntary leavers
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Targeted compensation and career intervention will address primary attrition drivers

diagnostic_signals.signal: Bench and training cost >8% of total payroll due to high replacement cycle
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Reducing attrition rate will directly lower training and bench cost

required_data_fields: Attrition data by band, function, tenure cohort, and manager (24 months)
Exit survey responses with departure reasons
Compensation benchmarking data (NASSCOM, Aon Hewitt, or equivalent)
Recruitment and onboarding cost per hire by band
Productivity ramp curve by role (time to full contribution)

condition_precedents: Seat occupancy data available (90+ days)
Hybrid work policy defined by parent
Lease renewal/exit windows identified
industry_sources: JLL India — GCCs Drive Record 77.2 mn sq ft Office Leasing in India (jll.com/en-in/newsroom/gccs-drive-record-77-2-mn-sqft-office-leasing-in-india)
Everest Group — Global Locations State of the Market 2024 (everestgrp.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/...)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Instrument all facilities with occupancy sensing (badge, Wi-Fi heatmap, or manual survey); measure peak and average utilisation by floor
Design hybrid work policy (3:2 or 4:1) aligned with parent standards; set seat-to-headcount ratio target (1 seat : 1.3 employees)
Consolidate underutilised floors/buildings; sublease or return surplus space to landlord at lease break or expiry
Track cost-per-seat, utilisation rate, and occupancy cost as % of total CTC quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Peak office utilisation <65% of installed seat capacity for ≥3 months
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Space rationalisation and sublease opportunity is material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Seat-to-headcount ratio <1.0 (more seats than employees) in a hybrid work environment
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Excess seat capacity can be released

diagnostic_signals.signal: Occupancy cost (rent, facilities, power) >10% of total payroll cost
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Seat optimisation will materially improve cost ratio

required_data_fields: Occupancy sensing data by floor and building (peak, average, and trend)
Seat count vs headcount by location and shift pattern
Lease terms, WALE, and break-clause positions by facility
Facilities cost breakdown (rent, power, housekeeping, security) per seat
Parent hybrid work policy requirements

condition_precedents: Process landscape across CoEs documented
Parent entity alignment on scope
Change management budget approved
ERP/workflow system compatibility confirmed
industry_sources: Accenture — Rise of the GCC as a Reinvention Engine (accenture.com/content/dam/accenture/final/accenture-com/document-2/Accenture-Rise-Of-GCC-As-A-Reinvention-Engine.pdf)
Everest Group — Beyond Boundaries: GCCs Shaping Tomorrow's Enterprises (December 2024)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Map F&A (AP, AR, GL, Payroll) and HR ops processes by BU; measure volume, FTE, and cost per transaction for each BU
Design SSC operating model: process scope, location, SLA framework, governance, and pricing/chargeback model
Migrate Wave 1 (transactional F&A) with parallel run for one period; transition staff under TUPE-equivalent arrangement
Stabilise SSC with SLA dashboard; enforce chargeback to BUs; migrate Wave 2 (HR ops, IT service desk)

diagnostic_signals.signal: Same transactional process (e.g., invoice processing) performed by >3 independent BU teams at different cost per transaction
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will reduce unit cost through scale and standardisation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cost per invoice processed >₹150 vs SSC benchmark <₹80 for equivalent volume
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scale economies from SSC are material

diagnostic_signals.signal: Multiple ERP instances or process variants across BUs for the same transactional sub-process
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Standardisation opportunity exists alongside consolidation

required_data_fields: Process inventory with volume, FTE, and cost per transaction by BU
ERP/technology landscape across business units
Current chargeback or recharge model for shared services
SLA benchmark data for comparable SSC operations
Location cost analysis (talent, real estate, infrastructure) for SSC hub

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Parent Recharge & Intercompany Transfer Pricing Rationalisation
condition_precedents: Transfer pricing study completed (last 3 years)
FEMA/RBI remittance structure documented
Tax counsel engaged on arm's-length pricing
Parent CFO alignment on recharge methodology
industry_sources: EY — Driving Transfer Pricing Certainty in Uncertain Times: 2024 International Tax and Transfer Pricing Survey (ey.com/en_in/insights/tax/international-tax-and-transfer-pricing-survey)
KPMG — Global Transfer Pricing Review (kpmg.com/us/en/articles/2023/global-transfer-pricing-review.html)
Deloitte — Transfer Pricing Controversy Trends: 2024 Global Survey (internationaltaxreview.com)

execution_playbook.step: Inventory all parent-to-GCC recharges: service type, volume driver, pricing basis, and contract; validate against actual services received
Benchmark each recharge category against arm's-length rate using TNMM or CUP method; identify over-priced or unsupported charges
Renegotiate service agreements to arm's-length pricing; eliminate charges for services not demonstrably received; update Ind AS 115 revenue recognition
Implement recharge governance dashboard; file updated Form 3CEB and TP documentation; track effective recharge rate quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Parent recharges as % of GCC revenue >12% for service categories that could be sourced locally at lower cost
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Repricing or scope reduction will reduce effective cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Recharge rates not benchmarked for >2 years despite changes in scope or market rates
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Benchmarking gap is creating over-payment risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: Recharge categories include 'management fee' or 'stewardship' without clear deliverables or usage metric
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Restructuring unsupported charges will reduce cost and TP risk

required_data_fields: Intercompany recharge schedule: service type, pricing basis, and volume driver
Intercompany service agreements with scope definition
Comparable market rates for each recharged service category
Form 3CEB and TP documentation
Ind AS 115 revenue recognition treatment for recharged services

condition_precedents: Vendor spend categorised and mapped
Contract renewal calendar built
Procurement authority clarified (India vs parent)
industry_sources: Gartner — Market Guide for Hyperautomation Service Providers (gartner.com/en/documents/4983531)
Everest Group — Chasing the GCC Opportunity: The Next Big Providers Play (everestgrp.com/report/egr-2025-34-r-7314/)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Identify GCC support functions (IT infrastructure, facilities management, security) with managed-services market alternatives and clear SLA definition
Benchmark managed-service pricing vs in-house total cost (FTE + infrastructure + overhead); confirm quality and risk profile
Run competitive RFP for selected managed-service scope; negotiate SLA-linked contracts with performance bonds
Transition in-house teams to vendor under TUPE-equivalent; track cost per unit of service and SLA compliance monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: In-house IT infrastructure or facilities support cost >20% above managed-service benchmark for equivalent coverage
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Managed-service shift will reduce cost with comparable service level

diagnostic_signals.signal: In-house support team utilisation <70% due to variable demand pattern
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Vendor with multi-client scale will improve resource efficiency

diagnostic_signals.signal: Support function SLA compliance below 95% with current in-house model for ≥2 quarters
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Specialised vendor will improve service quality alongside cost reduction

required_data_fields: In-house support function FTE count, cost, and utilisation by service line
Managed-service pricing benchmarks for IT infrastructure, facilities, and security
SLA performance data (uptime, response time, resolution time)
Existing vendor contracts and performance data
TUPE-equivalent obligations and transfer requirements

condition_precedents: Role-skill matrix defined
L&D budget baseline established
Parent charter supports internal mobility
industry_sources: WEF — Future of Jobs Report 2025 (weforum.org/publications/the-future-of-jobs-report-2025/)
Deloitte — 2025 Global Human Capital Trends (deloitte.com/us/en/insights/topics/talent/human-capital-trends/2025.html)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Confirm scope for Talent Pyramid Rebalancing & Upskilling: validate baseline and stakeholders
Design target process and RACI
Pilot in one BU/geo and measure effort/cost delta
Scale rollout and update SOPs/training

diagnostic_signals.signal: High FTE effort or cycle time vs benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process inefficiency is material

required_data_fields: Process volume metrics
FTE effort or cost allocation by process
Baseline cycle times

condition_precedents: Cloud usage and cost tagging baseline completed
FinOps practice or cloud COE established
Parent entity cloud strategy aligned
Application rationalization roadmap in place
industry_sources: FinOps Foundation — State of FinOps 2025 (data.finops.org/2025-report/)

McKinsey — Projecting the Global Value of Cloud: $3 Trillion is Up for Grabs (mckinsey.com/capabilities/tech-and-ai/our-insights/projecting-the-global-value-of-cloud-3-trillion-is-up-for-grabs-for-companies-that-go-beyond-adoption)

execution_playbook.step: Assess cloud maturity: map all workloads (on-prem, co-location, cloud) to cost, utilisation, and retirement risk; define migration priority
Build cloud migration roadmap: rehost, replatform, refactor, or retire by workload; establish landing zone and FinOps governance
Execute migration in waves (low-risk dev/test first, then production); enforce tagging and budget alerts from day 1
Implement FinOps: rightsizing recommendations, reserved instances, and savings plans; track cloud spend per BU monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: On-premises infrastructure utilisation <40% average with peak demand requiring expensive burst capacity
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Cloud migration will reduce idle infrastructure cost and improve elasticity

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cloud spend growing >30% YoY without corresponding workload growth, indicating waste
diagnostic_signals.confirms: FinOps governance will reduce cloud waste

diagnostic_signals.signal: Infrastructure cost per FTE >₹2 L/year vs cloud-optimised GCC benchmark of <₹1.2 L/year
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Cloud modernisation will improve cost per FTE ratio

required_data_fields: Workload inventory (on-prem, co-location, cloud) with cost and utilisation metrics
Cloud billing breakdown by service type, environment, and business unit
Network connectivity and security requirements for cloud migration
FinOps maturity assessment and tagging hygiene status
Parent/HQ cloud strategy and vendor preference

condition_precedents: Bench headcount tracked separately in HRIS
Demand pipeline from parent entity available (rolling 12 weeks)
Redeployment policy and skill tagging framework in place
industry_sources: Everest Group — Beyond Boundaries: GCCs Shaping Tomorrow's Enterprises (December 2024)
Accenture — Rise of the GCC as a Reinvention Engine (accenture.com/content/dam/accenture/final/accenture-com/document-2/Accenture-Rise-Of-GCC-As-A-Reinvention-Engine.pdf)

execution_playbook.step: Profile bench by skill, band, and bench tenure; classify as project-ramp (0–4 weeks), skill-gap (4–8 weeks), or structural bench (>8 weeks)
Implement internal mobility marketplace: match bench talent to open positions across BUs before external hiring
Redeploy skill-gap bench into upskilling programs (GenAI, cloud, data) with time-bound deployment commitment
Exit or PIP structural bench (>8 weeks, no deployment path); track bench % of total headcount and bench cost monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Bench headcount as % of total GCC headcount >8% for ≥2 consecutive quarters
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Bench reduction will directly lower payroll cost per billable FTE

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average bench tenure >6 weeks indicating deployment process or skill-market mismatch
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Structural bench indicates resource management process gap

diagnostic_signals.signal: External hiring ongoing for skills that exist in the bench profile
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Internal mobility process is broken; fixing it will reduce recruitment cost

required_data_fields: Bench headcount by skill, band, and bench tenure (weekly snapshots)
Open position inventory by skill and expected start date
External hiring pipeline and job requisitions
Training and upskilling program catalog
Bench cost (CTC for undeployed FTEs) per quarter

condition_precedents: Activity-based costing (ABC) model for GCC processes completed
Parent business unit leadership aligned on value-add vs. low-value activity split
GCC charter reviewed and signed off by parent CFO
industry_sources: McKinsey — Global Capability Centers: From Cost to Innovation (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/beyond-cost-savings-the-global-footprint-of-innovation-hubs)
Accenture — Rise of the GCC as a Reinvention Engine (accenture.com/content/dam/accenture/final/accenture-com/document-2/Accenture-Rise-Of-GCC-As-A-Reinvention-Engine.pdf)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Inventory all work scopes migrated to GCC: service line, volume, FTE assigned, and value-add classification (transactional vs strategic)
Assess each scope for value-for-money vs parent expectation: cost, quality, and strategic contribution vs local/other-vendor alternative
Right-size scope: return commoditised or low-value work that is cheaper locally; pitch for higher-value strategic scope
Track scope mix (transactional vs strategic), cost per output, and parent satisfaction score quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Transactional scope >60% of total GCC work portfolio, limiting value-add perception and long-term sustainability
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scope upgrade towards higher-value work will improve parent ROI and GCC resilience

diagnostic_signals.signal: Per-seat cost of GCC trending above offshore benchmark due to cost inflation without corresponding scope upgrade
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Scope rationalisation and value upgrade needed to justify cost premium

diagnostic_signals.signal: Parent considering in-sourcing or alternative vendor for ≥2 GCC service lines
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Proactive scope and value review is urgently needed

required_data_fields: GCC scope inventory with service line, FTE, and value-add classification
Cost per seat and per output by service line
Parent satisfaction scores and strategic expectation documentation
Competitive landscape: alternative delivery options by scope category
GCC 3-year roadmap and parent alignment

condition_precedents: Perks & benefits spend benchmarked vs NASSCOM/Mercer India data
Employee engagement baseline survey completed
CHRO and parent HR alignment on total rewards philosophy
industry_sources: Mercer — Total Remuneration Survey, India Edition (imercer.com/products/total-remuneration-surveys)
Deloitte — 2025 Global Human Capital Trends (deloitte.com/us/en/insights/topics/talent/human-capital-trends/2025.html)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Confirm scope for Employee Perks & Benefits Rationalisation: validate baseline and stakeholders
Design target process and RACI
Pilot in one BU/geo and measure effort/cost delta
Scale rollout and update SOPs/training

diagnostic_signals.signal: High FTE effort or cycle time vs benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process inefficiency is material

required_data_fields: Process volume metrics
FTE effort or cost allocation by process
Baseline cycle times

condition_precedents: GenAI governance policy approved by parent entity
Data classification and residency assessment completed
Pilot with ≥1 CoE completed (≥90 days, ≥30 FTEs)
Parent entity AI strategy and tooling selection confirmed
industry_sources: NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)
Accenture — Rise of the GCC as a Reinvention Engine (accenture.com/content/dam/accenture/final/accenture-com/document-2/Accenture-Rise-Of-GCC-As-A-Reinvention-Engine.pdf)
Everest Group — Beyond Boundaries: GCCs Shaping Tomorrow's Enterprises (December 2024)

execution_playbook.step: Conduct GenAI use-case discovery across top-5 functions (F&A, HR, IT, analytics, software development); estimate productivity impact per use case
Pilot highest-impact use cases (code generation, document summarisation, RFP response, data analysis) with volunteer teams; measure time savings
Scale proven pilots via standardised prompt libraries, tool integrations (Copilot, Claude, Gemini), and change management
Track productivity metrics (output per FTE, cycle time reduction) and report FTE capacity release to parent quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Functions with >40% of FTE time on document/data processing tasks where GenAI augmentation is proven (coding, legal review, financial analysis)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: GenAI adoption will yield measurable productivity improvement

diagnostic_signals.signal: Software development cycle time (story to deployment) >4 weeks vs benchmark <2 weeks in comparable GCCs with AI-assisted coding
diagnostic_signals.confirms: AI-assisted code generation will improve engineering throughput

diagnostic_signals.signal: Licence or subscription cost for GenAI tools already incurred but adoption <30% of eligible workforce
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Driving adoption of existing tools will improve ROI without incremental cost

required_data_fields: Function-level time allocation by task type (creative, analytical, document, coding)
Existing GenAI tool subscriptions and adoption rates
Software development metrics (velocity, cycle time, defect rate)
Parent GenAI policy and data-handling guidelines
Pilot design framework and measurement methodology

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Tier-2 City Location Strategy & Hub-and-Spoke Migration
condition_precedents: Role-by-role location sensitivity analysis completed
Parent entity approval for geographic expansion obtained
Attrition risk assessment for incumbent employees completed
STPI/SEZ registration feasibility confirmed for target city
industry_sources: NASSCOM — India GCC Landscape Report: The 5-Year Journey, co-published with Zinnov (zinnov.com/centers-of-excellence/zinnov-nasscom-india-gcc-landscape-report-the-5-year-journey-report/)

Everest Group — Global Locations State of the Market 2024 (everestgrp.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Everest-Group-Global-Locations-State-of-the-Market-2024-Key-Trends-Shaping-the-Landscape-Complimentary-Abstract.pdf)
JLL India — GCCs Drive Record 77.2 mn sq ft Office Leasing in India (jll.com/en-in/newsroom/gccs-drive-record-77-2-mn-sqft-office-leasing-in-india)

execution_playbook.step: Score Tier-2 cities against criteria: talent pool depth (technical graduates), infrastructure, attrition benchmarks, real-estate cost, and SEZ/STPI availability
Assess SEZ/STPI eligibility for target city; model 5-year TCO (salaries, real estate, infrastructure, incentives) vs Tier-1 incumbent location
Establish satellite office or CoE in selected Tier-2 city; migrate new hiring and selected existing functions over 12–18 months
Track cost per FTE (Tier-2 vs Tier-1), attrition rate, and talent quality metrics by location quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: GCC cost per FTE in current Tier-1 city growing >10% CAGR for 3 years, outpacing productivity improvement
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Tier-2 location will arrest cost escalation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Tier-2 salary benchmark for equivalent roles 25–35% below current Tier-1 location
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Material cost-per-FTE reduction is achievable via geographic arbitrage

diagnostic_signals.signal: Attrition rate in current Tier-1 location >20% driven by competition for talent from large tech employers
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Tier-2 talent market is less competitive, supporting retention improvement

required_data_fields: Cost per FTE comparison across Tier-1 and Tier-2 locations (salary, real estate, infrastructure)
Talent pool data: NASSCOM city report or equivalent for target Tier-2 cities
Attrition benchmarks by location
SEZ/STPI availability and incentive structure for target city
Real estate availability and cost per sq ft in Tier-2 candidate cities

condition_precedents: Big 4 tax counsel engaged for SEZ/STPI eligibility assessment
GCC operating from an SEZ notified zone or STPI registered unit
Transfer pricing structure aligned with SEZ deduction requirements
MAT credit entitlement and utilisation schedule reviewed
industry_sources: EY — Driving Transfer Pricing Certainty in Uncertain Times: 2024 International Tax and Transfer Pricing Survey (ey.com/en_in/insights/tax/international-tax-and-transfer-pricing-survey)
KPMG — Global Transfer Pricing Review (kpmg.com/us/en/articles/2023/global-transfer-pricing-review.html)
Deloitte — Transfer Pricing Controversy Trends: 2024 Global Survey (internationaltaxreview.com)

execution_playbook.step: Audit current SEZ/STPI status and compliance: unit approval, Softex declaration volume and coverage, and bond utilisation
Calculate IT/ITeS income tax exemption foregone due to non-filing or incomplete Softex declarations; quantify IEC and bond optimisation opportunity
File backdated Softex declarations (where permissible); correct STPI/SEZ compliance gaps and implement automated Softex filing workflow
Integrate Softex filing into monthly invoice generation workflow; track exemption utilisation and compliance calendar

diagnostic_signals.signal: Softex declaration coverage <80% of eligible software export invoices
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Filing gap recovery will immediately improve tax exemption position

diagnostic_signals.signal: IT income tax exemption utilised <90% of eligible income under STPI/SEZ scheme
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Compliance improvement will reduce effective tax rate

diagnostic_signals.signal: STPI/SEZ bond amount not optimised (excess bond increasing opportunity cost)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Bond rightsizing will release working capital

required_data_fields: Softex declaration log vs total software export invoice value
IT exemption claimed vs eligible income in tax returns
STPI/SEZ unit approval documents and scope
Bond register and utilisation rate
Monthly software export invoice data by client and currency

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Chargeback Model Transformation — Cost-Plus to Value-Based Billing
condition_precedents: Service catalogue with defined SLAs and output metrics in place
External market rate benchmarking completed (Everest/NASSCOM data)
Parent CFO and business unit heads aligned on billing model change
Transfer pricing implications reviewed with tax counsel
industry_sources: Accenture — Rise of the GCC as a Reinvention Engine (accenture.com/content/dam/accenture/final/accenture-com/document-2/Accenture-Rise-Of-GCC-As-A-Reinvention-Engine.pdf)
Everest Group — Beyond Boundaries: GCCs Shaping Tomorrow's Enterprises (December 2024)
McKinsey — Global Capability Centers: From Cost to Innovation (mckinsey.com/capabilities/operations/our-insights/beyond-cost-savings-the-global-footprint-of-innovation-hubs)

execution_playbook.step: Audit current recharge model: map each GCC cost pool to chargeback driver, rate, and receiving BU; identify misalignments vs actual consumption
Redesign to activity-based costing model: define unit rates per service line (cost per FTE, per transaction, per project hour) with transparent overhead allocation
Align new chargeback model with parent finance teams; obtain sign-off and integrate into ERP intercompany billing module
Publish monthly chargeback dashboards by BU and service line; track demand discipline and cost recovery rate

diagnostic_signals.signal: Recharge disputes from BUs >3 per quarter due to lack of transparency in cost allocation
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Activity-based costing model will improve transparency and reduce disputes

diagnostic_signals.signal: GCC cost recovery rate <95% of budget due to incorrect chargeback basis or BU pushback
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Model redesign will improve cost recovery and GCC P&L accuracy

diagnostic_signals.signal: BU decision-making on GCC scope driven by perception of value rather than actual cost data
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Transparent unit-cost pricing will enable rational demand decisions

required_data_fields: Current chargeback rate card and allocation methodology by cost pool
GCC P&L by service line and function
BU demand data by service type and volume
Intercompany billing configuration in ERP
Parent finance accounting policy for intercompany services

condition_precedents: Org structure mapped with spans and layers analysed
Parent HR policy on grade/band equivalence reviewed
Attrition risk for affected managers assessed
industry_sources: Deloitte — 2025 Global Human Capital Trends (deloitte.com/us/en/insights/topics/talent/human-capital-trends/2025.html)
WEF — Future of Jobs Report 2025 (weforum.org/publications/the-future-of-jobs-report-2025/)
Mercer — Total Remuneration Survey, India Edition (imercer.com/products/total-remuneration-surveys)

execution_playbook.step: Analyse GCC org chart: compute average span of control and management layer count by function vs NASSCOM GCC benchmark
Define target spans (IC-to-manager: 8–12 for execution roles, 5–8 for strategic roles) and maximum layer count (4 from GCC CEO to IC)
Redesign org structure: flatten layers, eliminate single-direct-report manager roles, and combine sub-scale teams
Implement change plan with redeployment or exit for displaced roles; track manager-to-IC ratio and cost-per-band quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average span of control <5 ICs per manager for non-specialist functions, indicating over-management
diagnostic_signals.confirms: De-layering will reduce management overhead cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Management layers >5 from GCC CEO to individual contributor for delivery functions
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Layer reduction will improve decision speed and reduce overhead ratio

diagnostic_signals.signal: Manager-to-IC ratio >1:4 with management cost as % of total payroll >30%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Flatter structure will improve cost efficiency

required_data_fields: Org chart with headcount and CTC by band and function
Span-of-control data by manager across all levels
NASSCOM or peer GCC benchmark for management structure
Single-direct-report manager inventory
Cost by band (manager vs IC) for overhead ratio calculation

condition_precedents: IT asset discovery tool deployed (Zylo, Torii, or equivalent)
Procurement policy mandating IT approval for SaaS purchases communicated
Shadow IT amnesty period defined before enforcement
industry_sources: FinOps Foundation — State of FinOps 2025 (data.finops.org/2025-report/)
Gartner — Market Guide for Hyperautomation Service Providers (gartner.com/en/documents/4983531)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Run SaaS discovery scan using browser plug-in or SSO logs; identify all active SaaS applications by BU and usage frequency
Compare against approved IT catalog; classify shadow IT into: approve+consolidate, migrate to approved alternative, or decommission
Negotiate consolidated licensing agreements for approved SaaS (Microsoft, Atlassian, Slack); enforce single-vendor SSO login to control proliferation
Track authorised vs shadow application count monthly; enforce procurement policy requiring IT review for new SaaS subscriptions

diagnostic_signals.signal: Shadow SaaS (unauthorised tools) representing >20% of total SaaS spend discovered through discovery scan
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rationalisation and governance will reduce waste and security risk

diagnostic_signals.signal: Duplicate SaaS tools for same function across BUs (e.g., multiple project management tools, multiple video conferencing subscriptions)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation to single approved tool will reduce per-seat cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: SaaS spend growing >30% YoY without corresponding headcount or output growth
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Governance implementation will arrest uncontrolled proliferation

required_data_fields: SaaS discovery report (SSO logs, browser plug-in scan, credit card statement analysis)
Approved IT catalog with standard tooling by function
SaaS contract and licence data (vendor, users, cost, renewal date)
Security risk classification for shadow IT applications
Procurement policy for software purchases

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Recruitment Cost Optimisation — Internal Sourcing & Direct Hiring
condition_precedents: Annual hiring plan and agency spend baseline established
HRIS supports direct application and referral tracking
Employer brand assessment completed (Glassdoor/LinkedIn benchmark)
industry_sources: Mercer — Total Remuneration Survey, India Edition (imercer.com/products/total-remuneration-surveys)
Deloitte — 2025 Global Human Capital Trends (deloitte.com/us/en/insights/topics/talent/human-capital-trends/2025.html)
NASSCOM — GCC Annual Report 2024 (community.nasscom.in)

execution_playbook.step: Confirm scope for Recruitment Cost Optimisation — Internal Sourcing & Direct Hiring: validate baseline and stakeholders
Design target process and RACI
Pilot in one BU/geo and measure effort/cost delta
Scale rollout and update SOPs/training

diagnostic_signals.signal: High FTE effort or cycle time vs benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Process inefficiency is material

required_data_fields: Process volume metrics
FTE effort or cost allocation by process
Baseline cycle times
